game_name: Dungeons & Dragons: Eye of the Beholder

danger_signals.visual_form: 3D rendered creature sprites in first-person perspective view
danger_signals.meaning: Direct threat requiring combat engagement; visual clarity of monster positioning and type informs threat assessment

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental destruction triggered by Xanathar's presence
danger_signals.meaning: Structural failure blocks retreat and signals powerful enemy presence; world-state danger manifests through architectural destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Descent through multiple dungeon levels (sewers → dwarf/drow clans → Xanathar's lair)
danger_signals.meaning: Visual geography increases danger markers; deeper levels correlate with greater threats and proximity to final boss

safety_signals.visual_form: Starting location above ground; questgiver NPCs present
safety_signals.meaning: Civilized space; adventure base and hiring point; absence of immediate combat threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly non-player characters encountered in dungeons
safety_signals.meaning: Party expansion opportunities; NPCs signal local 'safe' interaction zones within otherwise hostile dungeons

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: City surface, quest-giving authority; represents law and civilization ordering the adventure
Dungeon level containing dwarf territory; distinct clan space in vertical dungeon hierarchy
Dungeon level containing drow territory; distinct from dwarf clan spatially; underground faction occupancy

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: The Beholder itself as central visual threat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Lair at dungeon bottom; cause of sewer collapse; primary antagonist; visual manifestation of world-threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: First-person view of city/quest location with NPC questgivers; 3D graphics rendering civilized space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party assembles and receives mission briefing; adventure formally begins

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dungeon entrance collapse blocks retreat; environmental destruction visible; descending levels become progressively darker/more dangerous
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced forward progression; no escape route; escalating difficulty; final confrontation becomes inevitable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct dungeon visual areas representing clan occupation; environmental theming changes per faction territory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different monster types and NPC encounters in each clan area; territorial encounters reflect faction presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Text notification in blue window (DOS version); later Amiga version added animated ending sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Party returns to surface as heroes; adventure concludes; reward state established through narrative closure

environmental_storytelling.example: Progression from surface (Waterdeep) → sewers → dwarf clan territory → drow clan territory → Xanathar's lair creates narrative structure; depth equals danger escalation and world-state change
Dwarf and drow clans occupy distinct dungeon levels; player's visual movement through these territories communicates faction territorial claims and political geography without explicit text
Sewer collapse caused by Xanathar blocks retreat; players experience this destruction visually and mechanically; world-state changes from 'exploration possible' to 'forced descent inevitable'
3D computer graphics rendering 'actual dungeon feeling' (per reviewer Scorpia); visual immediacy of combat, monster positioning, and spatial layout creates emotional world-state communication

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Squad composition variable; can recruit NPCs and resurrect skeletons to expand party size; flexibility signals strategic depth and world-state adaptability
Found throughout dungeons; offer party expansion; can be resurrected as undead; recruitment signals safe interaction zones within hostile environment
Engaged via point-and-click combat interface; variety suggests faction/territory-specific threats; combat behavior follows turn-based dungeon crawler rules
Causes dungeon collapse blocking escape; forces party descent deeper; final lair confrontation; behavior manifests as world-altering event (sewer destruction)

notes: Source material provides plot and gameplay overview but limited explicit detail on visual manifestation specifics. Danger/safety signals extracted from world-state narrative (surface vs. dungeon depth, collapse event). Faction indicators inferred from territorial occupancy described in plot. Environmental storytelling reconstructed from vertical progression structure and catastrophic event mechanics. Reviewers emphasized that VGA graphics and 'actual dungeon feeling' created immersive world-state communication, though specific visual techniques not detailed in source. Data Tier T3 qualitative assessment appropriate given available sources.
